segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with an upbeat, slightly tense background music as a female voiceover declares, "Fast food is everywhere." Visually, a young child with curly hair in a high chair eats fries with a fork, a purple sippy cup and McDonald’s box on the tray. The scene quickly shifts to a happy infant in a blue bib being spoon-fed, followed by a young boy in a blue sweater sitting on a black leather couch, looking at the camera. A male voice, likely a parent, describes this boy, stating, "He likes KFC, chicken nuggets, kebabs, fish and chips," and later adds, "he does love fizzy pop," consuming "probably on average say six a day," admitting, "I've got no control of what he eats." A woman with dark hair and a black top, speaking directly to the camera in a warmly lit room, adds, "Anything that's bad for you, he'll eat." The female voiceover continues, "Our kids just can't get enough of it." The visuals show a child in a gray sweater being fed a burger in a car, then another child in a high chair with fries and a chicken nugget. A child in a bright yellow hoodie is seen holding a red Coca-Cola bottle in a grocery aisle, visually reinforcing the "fizzy pop" comment. A woman in a red polka-dot top speaks in a kitchen, followed by a close-up of a hand reaching into a refrigerator filled with snacks and a bottle of alcohol. A plate of golden, crispy fries is shown. The woman in the black top, speaking again, lists the health consequences: "I see tooth decay, iron deficiency anemia, rickets even," and later, "They are a recipe for heart disease, diabetes." A child's voice briefly calls out "Nona!" The female voiceover warns, "It's a ticking time bomb of massive health problems." Visually, the woman in the black top is seen being comforted on a couch by another woman in a colorful scarf. The video returns to a child in a high chair with a blue bib being spoon-fed, and a group of people eating around a table, with a woman in a red top reaching for food. The female voiceover concludes, "We meet three families with three different fast food problems," as the segment ends with a close-up of a plate of fried food, possibly fish and chips, with a hand dipping a piece into ketchup, while the background music continues.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of a plate of golden-brown fried food, as a narrator introduces "a clan of takeaway addicts whose diet is shared by even their tiniest tot." This transitions to a kitchen where a young child with curly hair, wearing a gray shirt, is seated in a high chair, actively eating fries and holding a fork, babbling and giggling while an adult gently tells them to be "still." The scene shifts to a dining table where a woman in a red long-sleeve top reaches for food, and a young boy in a blue sweater eats from a bowl, as the narrator introduces him as a "19-month-old whose mum and dad are frightened by his refusal to eat healthy food." The video then cuts to a child with curly hair, wearing a gray sweater, being fed a burger in a car seat, identified by an adult voice calling out "Michael, eat, Michael!" amidst the child's distressed cries. Following this, a child in a bright yellow hoodie is seen holding a red Coca-Cola bottle in a grocery store aisle, introduced by the narrator as "a three-year-old whose mum has never cooked him a healthy meal," and heard asking for "a small doner meat with chips, with cheese and mayonnaise." The narrator emphasizes that "all the families desperately want help" and are "teaming up with experts to see if their babies can kick the habit." In a living room, a woman with dark hair and a black top, who is also seen being comforted by another woman in a colorful scarf, speaks to the camera, expressing her need to be "reined back in" and "start from scratch" with expert help to change her habits. The segment concludes with a woman in a red polka-dot top speaking in a kitchen, as the narrator reiterates that "19-month-old Cuba Fish from Cardiff is a fast food baby," all set against a backdrop of warm, intimate family moments and an underlying concern for healthy eating habits.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with upbeat, slightly quirky background music as a young child named Cuba, with curly hair and wearing a gray shirt, is seen in a high chair with a red patterned backrest, eating fries and holding a fork. A woman with dark hair, wearing a black top, speaks to the camera from a home setting with floral wallpaper and a window, describing someone's love for McDonald's, KFC, and Indian food. A man's voice, later identified as Simon, adds that 'he' also likes pizzas and chips with plenty of salt. A female narrator then introduces the central theme: 'Cuba has one big problem. His whole family are takeaway addicts.' The woman in the black top elaborates on their frequent takeaway habit, stating they have it 'Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday night takeaway.' The scene transitions to a child named Shay, in a gray sweater, being fed a burger in a car seat. A child's voice, possibly Cuba's, says 'Thank you,' to which Simon replies, 'You're welcome,' and then remarks, 'Been ordering for years.' The video shows a baby in a blue bib being spoon-fed happily, followed by a young boy in a blue cardigan sitting on a black leather couch, holding a red Coca-Cola bottle. A child's voice exclaims, 'Ooh, chips!', and another child, likely Cuba, declares, 'I'm ready for Cuba,' with Simon confirming, 'Cuba.' The female narrator's voice returns, detailing that 'The takeaways are soaked in harmful saturated fat,' and that 'Cuba's consuming half a liter of oil a week.' A woman in a red polka-dot top is seen speaking in a kitchen with a refrigerator in the background. Simon's voice asks, 'So what do you have in there, Shay?', and Shay replies, 'I have chips and peas,' which Simon confirms. The narrator then identifies 'Construction assessor Simon' as a 'driving force behind this fast food feeding.' The video briefly shows a woman in a black top sitting on a couch being comforted by another woman wearing a colorful scarf, and a woman in a red long-sleeve top reaching for food at a dining table with others. Simon, the man in the black shirt, is then seen placing food on a plate for Cuba in a high chair, followed by Simon and a woman in a black top preparing food in a kitchen. The background music continues as Simon, driving a car, approaches a McDonald's restaurant, with the iconic golden arches visible in the distance, and is heard asking, 'Are we hungry still?', to which a child replies, 'Yes.'

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a family in a car, where a child named Cuba, with curly hair and wearing a gray sweater, is being fed a burger from a white takeout container by an adult's hand, while another child sits beside them. A male voice, identified by a narrator as Simon, the father, is heard stating he takes the kids to McDonald's on Friday and Saturday. The scene transitions to Simon, a man in a black jacket, driving the car, and then speaking directly to the camera in a kitchen setting, describing Cuba's "very big cute smile." A child's voice, likely Cuba, exclaims "It's hot," followed by Simon's voice noting, "It's burning, he needs some sauce." The family then moves to the kitchen, where a woman, identified by the narrator as Sam, Cuba's mum, wearing a blue and white striped shirt, is cooking, adding ingredients to a pot on the stove, while a young girl in a pink top watches. The young child Cuba, now in a high chair with a red patterned backrest, is being fed fries by Sam, who is now in a red polka-dot top. Sam is heard speaking about her pasta, which she feels is like "shop-bought pasta" and complains that "nobody eats it," asking if "anybody want this pasta?" to which Simon replies "No." Laughter from multiple people is heard during this exchange. The family gathers around a dining table, with Simon holding a baby in a blue bib, and Cuba eating fries. Sam, in the striped shirt, is later seen preparing food at the counter, then cleaning up by throwing trash into a bin. The scene shifts to the living room, where Sam vacuums the carpet while Cuba plays with a toy vacuum. The video ends with Sam folding clothes on a wooden table, with a child in a blue cardigan sitting nearby, as background music begins to play. The narrator concludes by stating Sam works two days a week as a banking consultant and spends the other five "meticulously" cleaning her home.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a cozy home kitchen, accompanied by upbeat, slightly quirky background music. A woman in a blue and white striped shirt cleans nearby as a young child with curly hair, wearing a light blue vest, playfully engages with a toy hammer on a glass table. A narrator's voice comments on avoiding dishes by eating out. The scene shifts to the kitchen sink where the woman loads a dish rack, while the child, identified by the narrator as "Cuba," explores cabinets, pulling out a green container, then stands by the open cabinet holding a small object as the woman crouches nearby. The narrator mentions that "Cuba and sisters Cleo and Shay also have free rein to plunder the sweetie cupboard." The video cuts to a man in a dark sweater speaking directly to the camera in a home setting. His voice is heard stating that the child eats "at least 30 times for the day" and that he has "no control of what he eats." A quick shot follows of the child, Cuba, in a high chair, eating fries with a fork. The narrator then remarks that "whilst the girls have learned self-control, Cuba hasn't," as the child makes babbling sounds. The toddler is later seen in a crib, standing and holding a blue pacifier, while a woman in a black top with a red belt interacts with them. The narrator describes Cuba as knocking "all the fast food back with an unbelievable amount of cola," up to "six cans a day," and declares "it's out of control" as the child laughs. The scene transitions to a woman in a white top sitting on a couch, laughing and gesturing. The narrator adds that "Cuba can't sleep and Sam is now worried about his health." A woman's voice, likely that of the woman on the couch, is heard saying she's "totally conscious of it now" and needs to "be reined back in and just start from scratch." The video returns to Cuba in the crib, now holding a bottle and pacifier, before ending with the child looking directly at the camera with a curious expression.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a woman in a white top and black feathered shawl sitting contemplatively on a plush brown couch, stating, "I just don't know where to start really, I think with her." The scene shifts to a kitchen where a man in a red sports jersey, identified by a narrator as Simon, feeds a young child with curly hair, named Sam, who is seated in a high chair eating fries. The narrator comments, "Sam is prepared for change. But is Simon?" Simon, now in a dark hoodie, speaks directly to the camera, admitting, "I'm not too sure about the fast food. Quite enjoy that. I think he enjoys that. So I wouldn't like to stop too much of that." Upbeat, slightly jazzy background music begins as the video transitions to an outdoor suburban street with parked cars and terraced houses, where a pigeon perches on a TV antenna. A narrator introduces "Jane Imperato," a woman in a red top and black jacket, carrying a blue bag and a black container, as she walks toward a house and enters through the front door. Inside, Jane, described as a "hugely experienced family health visitor" who has "spent 20 years coercing families onto healthy diets," is seen speaking to the camera, asserting, "I think that I am reasonably firm. I think I'm a good parent... to my parents." She then interacts with a baby, identified as Cuba, in a blue bib, feeding him with a spoon. Jane plays with colorful building blocks on the floor with Cuba, saying "Hi there" and "You're doing it" as the baby coos, and later exclaiming, "Look what we've got here. Let's have a look at those. Wow, those are pretty, aren't they?" while Cuba makes happy sounds. Another woman in a black top sits nearby. The two women then sit on the couch, engaging in conversation in the cozy, softly lit living room, with Cuba playing on the floor between them. The narrator states, "Cuba has no choice in what he eats. To crack his problem, Jane will have to crack the whole family." Jane addresses the other woman, asking, "Tell me all about what's been happening... and what you feel your main issues are."

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of Cuba, an infant in a gray vest, white shirt, and blue jeans, lying on a plush brown couch with a blue pacifier in his mouth. A soft, slightly melancholic background music begins as a female narrator explains, "In the UK, one in eight toddlers are now iron deficient. This can, in the worst cases, slow a child's development," adding that "Cuba will need a blood test to see if he's anemic." The camera then pulls back to reveal a living room setting where Jane, a woman with dark hair in a black top, is seated on a couch, conversing with another woman in a colorful scarf and dark top. The woman in the scarf asks, "How long's it been going on?" to which a voice (likely Jane's) replies, "Frankly as long as he's been born." The scene transitions to Cuba sitting on a beige carpet, playing with colorful building blocks, while the woman in the colorful scarf interacts with him, offering a toy. A detailed shot of a large, ornate dollhouse is shown before the setting shifts to a kitchen. Here, Sam, a man in a black hoodie with neon green drawstrings, holds Cuba, who is then passed between Sam and Jane. Jane is heard asserting, "We need to start cooking in this household. I want them on three meals a day." Sam interjects, "Sam, cooking," and Jane clarifies, "It's not, no, it's not all about Sam. I I I need you to back Sam up. When you're looking after the children, I need you to cook for them." Sam asks, "Are you against this?" and Jane responds, "Yes, you are. You can cook, Sam. You can cook." Sam replies, "It's just getting the time yourself, obviously," with Jane adding, "Yeah, working from home." Cuba is then placed on a kitchen counter, leaning over a metal bowl. The woman in the colorful scarf reappears, holding a small orange fruit, and interacts with Cuba, asking Jane, "And were you saying that you didn't like the mess involved in cooking?" to which Jane replies, "Oh, the mess he makes, it's just irritating." The atmosphere throughout is warm and familial, with soft lighting and a focus on nurturing interactions between the adults and Cuba, who remains the central figure, consistently wearing the same outfit and blue pacifier.

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens in a warmly lit, cozy home where a family of three is gathered around a wooden dining table. Gareth, an adult man in a black long-sleeve shirt, pours Coca-Cola from a red bottle into a glass. Across from him, Kara, an adult woman in a cream-colored textured sweater, eats from a bowl of vegetables and potatoes. Their young child, Michael, with light hair and wearing a blue and gray striped shirt and jeans, sits in a wooden high chair, eating from a white bowl with a spoon. Michael soon becomes distressed, crying loudly and whimpering, pushing his bowl away. Kara attempts to comfort him, saying "Yes. Eat your dinner," and "Come on," while Gareth also tries to soothe him, calling out "Michael, Michael." A female narrator explains that "the more they try and control his eating... the more Michael rebels." Kara firmly tells Michael, "No, you don't do that. You don't do that," and Gareth adds, "Very naughty." Gareth then stands, gently interacts with the crying Michael, and lifts him from the high chair, carrying him into a hallway as Michael continues to cry. A close-up shows Kara's concerned face. Michael is then seen standing in a living room, still crying amidst toys and a play tent. Gareth reappears, picks up Michael again, and walks down the hallway, with Kara following. Kara, sounding exasperated, comments, "This is what it's like every mealtime," and later reflects that the situation is "affecting Kara and Gareth's relationship," causing them to "be stressed each other because of what's going on and having to clean up all the food." She concludes, "it's a stressful time," before firmly telling Michael, "Michael, sit down, please!"

A man in a black long-sleeve shirt pours Coca-Cola from a red bottle into a glass while a woman in a cream-colored textured sweater sits across from him, eating from a bowl of vegetables and potatoes. A young child with light hair, wearing a blue and gray striped shirt and jeans, sits in a wooden high chair, eating from a white bowl with a spoon. The child appears distressed, crying and pushing the bowl away, prompting the woman to reach out and comfort him. The man stands up, gently interacts with the child, and then lifts him from the chair. The child continues to cry as the man carries him away from the dining area into a hallway. The scene shifts to a close-up of the woman’s face, showing concern, then cuts to the child standing in a living room, still crying, with toys and a play tent visible in the background. The man reappears, picks up the child, and walks down the hallway, with the woman following behind. The environment is cozy and domestic, with family photos on the windowsill and children’s toys scattered around, suggesting a lived-in, nurturing home. The lighting is soft and natural, coming from a window behind the table, creating a calm yet emotionally charged atmosphere as the family responds to the child’s distress.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a cozy living room where Gareth, a man in a black hoodie, carries his young son, Michael, who wears a blue and gray striped shirt. Gareth announces, "Lunchtime!" and Michael responds with happy, excited sounds. Narration explains that "Every meal ends the same way. Caron and Gareth resort to fast food feeding," noting they live directly above a fish and chip shop. Gareth exits into a suburban street, walking towards 'FISH & FINS'. Inside the shop, a woman in a blue uniform serves a customer. Gareth orders, "Hi. Um, can I get a regular cod and small chips please?" A female voice, implied to be Caron, narrates, expressing, "When I give him fast food, I do feel like a bad parent... but if he's eating something... that's better than having nothing." Gareth receives the food, thanks the shopkeeper, "Thank you very much, Steve. Bye," and she replies, "Bye." Back in the living room, Gareth narrates, "We've got to feed him... you've got to give him something, haven't you?" Michael, now in a fish-design onesie, happily eats fish and chips on the black leather couch, making joyful sounds. Narration highlights that "Michael is one toddler who knows how to get exactly what he wants." The scene shifts to a grocery store, where Gareth pushes Michael in a shopping cart. Gareth says, "Here you go, Mikey. Shall I put them in the trolley? Put them in the trolley first," as he picks up a yellow bag of snacks that Michael, now in a blue jacket, reaches for. The video returns to the living room, where Michael continues to happily eat snacks, making contented sounds, with Gareth smiling beside him, emphasizing the warmth of their family life.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with an upbeat, slightly dramatic background music as a female narrator states that "Kiba in Cardiff" clocks up "a whopping 3,000 calories" daily. Visually, a young child with curly hair, wearing a gray shirt, is seen actively eating fries with a fork from a white plate in a red patterned high chair. The scene then shifts to a happy baby in a blue bib and white shirt being spoon-fed. The music becomes more somber as the narrator explains, "Michael's bad habits began after he became seriously ill." A wider shot reveals a living room where a woman with dark hair in a black top sits on a couch, while a man with short light brown hair in a black long-sleeve shirt and jeans sits beside her. A young child with light hair, wearing a blue cardigan, eats from a plate on a black leather couch. The woman in black is seen nurturing a baby in a white outfit, while the man pours Coca-Cola from a red plastic bottle into a glass. An emotional woman's voice, likely the mother, recounts the terrifying experience of "Michael" contracting meningitis at six months old, with doctors calling it "very, very serious," leading them to "prepare ourselves and think the worst." This is accompanied by close-ups of golden, crispy fries and a plate of golden fried food, likely fish and chips, being served and eaten by the child. A woman in a red long-sleeve top reaches for food at a dining table. The emotional man's voice, likely the father, adds that it was "very stressful" and they were told Michael was "nearly dying," followed by a distressed sigh. The woman's emotional voice continues, describing the heartbreak of a child going through such an ordeal. Amidst this, a young child's voice, possibly Michael's, exclaims "Eat up!" The man in black shirt is then seen comforting and carrying a crying child, while the woman in black top is comforted by another woman on a couch with patterned cushions. A curious baby in a gray vest, white shirt, and blue jeans is seen on an island, then held by a man in a black hoodie. This man is later seen carrying a young child in a blue and gray striped shirt through the living room, which is filled with a colorful play tent and scattered toys, before returning home with food from a shop. The woman's emotional voice concludes, reflecting that Michael's illness "definitely affected how we behave with Michael because we give in to him. We just like spoil him a bit more." The video ends with the child in the blue and gray striped shirt eating fish and chips from a plate on the black leather couch, as a woman in a cream-colored sweater attempts to comfort the crying child at the dining table, and a wooden high chair with a woven seat is visible. The somber background music continues throughout.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with Cara, a woman with dark hair and a black top, looking directly at the camera from a doorway, as a reflective female voiceover states, "We haven't had many choices." This transitions to an extreme close-up of a young child with striking blue eyes, later identified as Michael, reaching out curiously, while the voiceover explains his past struggle: "When most babies are starting to come off milk onto a normal diet, Michael was fighting for his life." Accompanied by somber, reflective music, the scene shifts outdoors to Cara pushing a stroller down a quiet suburban street, heading towards the 'Meadow Sure Start Children’s Centre,' which the voiceover describes as "Michael's toddler group" and "a walk of shame" for Cara. Inside the bright, child-friendly center, children's chatter and the clatter of dishes fill the air as several toddlers, including Michael, are seated around a low table, eating chopped vegetables and snacks from colorful plastic bowls and plates. Cara, now actively supervising, explains in a voiceover that while "all the other children have taken some fruit and vegetables... and they're all eating them," she "always have to bring stuff with me to put on his plate otherwise he won't eat what the other children are eating." She is seen helping children, including asking one, "Have you eaten your rice?" to which a child replies "No," and directly encouraging Michael, "Come and eat some fruit, Michael! Come and sit down and eat your fruit," highlighting the nurturing yet challenging environment focused on early childhood development and community support.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with a close-up of the Woman with dark hair and black top, later identified as Kara, speaking directly to the camera in a dimly lit room, expressing her deep embarrassment as a mum about giving her son sweets, crisps, and chocolate because he refuses healthy food. Upbeat, slightly dramatic background music begins as the scene transitions to a still image of a framed photograph showing a smiling woman holding a baby on a mantelpiece, alongside a small yellow toy, evoking family memories. This is followed by a serene sunset shot, casting a warm orange glow. A narrator then states that 'Kara and Gareth are desperate for help,' and are meeting Dr. Catherine Dendy, ex-head of feeding at Great Ormond Street Children's Hospital. The scene shifts to a suburban street at dusk, where the Woman in red long-sleeve top, revealed to be Catherine, walks past a 'Fish & Chips' shop and parked cars before entering a warmly lit home. A doorbell rings, and Catherine is greeted by a child's voice. She introduces herself as 'Catherine,' and a man's voice responds, 'Nice to meet you.' The camera follows Catherine into a cozy living room filled with toys and a play tent, where a baby, referred to as Michael, in a red outfit lies on the floor, reaching for a colorful book. The narrator explains Catherine's role as a clinical psychologist. Catherine then gestures expressively as she speaks to Kara, who is seated on a black leather couch, explaining she's there 'to see Michael having a meal' and to 'experience with you what happens,' asking them to 'do what you would normally do.' Kara responds with an affirmative sound. The narrator states Catherine observes a typical mealtime. The final scene shows a family meal at a wooden dining table, with Catherine serving food, Kara seated beside her, and Michael, the child in a high chair, eating and making sounds, while a man in a striped shirt serves drinks. Kara asks, 'Carrot?' amidst the clattering of dishes.

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens in a warmly lit, lived-in living room, where a family is sharing a meal around a wooden dining table. A young child, identified as Michael, in a red long-sleeve shirt, is seated, making soft 'goo-goo' sounds and a distinct 'clink' as he reaches into a bowl of food. A woman with dark hair and a black top, later identified as Karen, sits beside him, gently prompting him with 'Tato' and 'Stop playing with your food. Put it in your mouth, it's yummy!' as he babbles and makes playful noises. A man in a striped shirt, Gareth, is also at the table, eating and interacting, occasionally saying 'No, no' to Michael's actions. In the background, a woman with short blonde hair in a purple top, Catherine, observes from a black leather couch. As Michael becomes fussy, making louder 'ah!' sounds and babbling, Karen lifts him from the high chair and carries him to the couch, where Catherine comforts him with a 'Shh!' and asks, 'Do you want to watch your program?' Michael is then placed on the couch, continuing to eat from a bowl while being watched over. A narrator's voice explains, 'As usual, Karen and Gareth end up giving in to Michael's demands and put him in his favorite spot on the sofa.' Catherine then begins a consultation, stating, 'It seems to me that what's happened to Michael is that this really, really important stage of weaning and building up the new tastes and the new textures...' The scene captures intimate family dynamics, focusing on Michael's needs and the adults' responsive care.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a cozy living room, focusing on a woman with dark hair in a black top and a man in a checkered shirt, seated intimately on a black leather couch. They share a relaxed conversation, with the man's arm around her. The scene then cuts to a woman with short blonde hair in a purple top, also on the couch, speaking with a concerned expression. She is heard discussing how someone has "been interrupted" and "been feeling really, really anxious about it," continuing to explain that "if he starts eating something, you say great, and you give him more of it," describing it as "like a circle." She emphasizes that "even if he's like the slightest bit, something's not right," and expresses a feeling of "oh, there's something wrong with him." The man briefly interjects with "Yeah, yes, inside," and "It's natural though, isn't it?" The woman in purple top continues, stating that "he is keen to put his hands into things and try them out." The camera returns to the couple, where the woman laughs and gestures playfully, and the man smiles, indicating a lighthearted moment. The woman in purple top reappears, speaking with a serious and reflective tone, recalling a phrase: "Don't play with your food, Michael." She elaborates, "I know we're all brought up not to play with our food," to which the man agrees, "That's right, yeah." She then asserts, "actually, what we want him to do is play with his food," adding that "his table manners will come later." A brief cut shows a young child in a red long-sleeve shirt quietly eating from a green bowl on the couch. The scene shifts back to the couple, now laughing and interacting warmly, with the woman leaning into the man. The woman in purple top concludes her thought with a more animated expression, as the camera pans slightly to reveal a radiator and a window ledge adorned with framed family photos, reinforcing the domestic and intimate atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of a man in a checkered shirt and a woman in a black top, seated closely on a black leather couch, smiling and engaging in conversation. The scene then shifts to a woman in a purple top, seated on a wooden chair, speaking animatedly about helping someone get used to fruit and veg, and doing it in a fun way. The man in the checkered shirt responds with agreement, saying, "Hopefully, yeah," and "That'd be great, wouldn't it, yeah." The camera returns to the couple on the couch, with the man's arm around the woman, both smiling, before panning to a wider view of the living room, revealing a child in a blue sweater on the couch, a play tent, and scattered toys, indicating a family-friendly space. A female narrator's voice then introduces background music, explaining that Catherine will refer Michael to a children's feeding expert, Lucy Thomas, for practical support, and work with Cara and Gareth on their anxieties. The visuals transition to a nighttime exterior of a house with a lit window, followed by a crescent moon in the dark sky, accompanied by the sound of a car driving. An arched bridge over a river with passing vehicles is shown, then a white seagull perched on a railing. The scene cuts to a daytime exterior of a brick house with the child in the blue sweater sitting on the porch. Inside, a woman in a white tank top is seen opening a pizza box on the black leather couch, while the child eats a slice. The woman's voice is heard asking the child, "Right, how many pieces of garlic bread do you want, mate?" and offering, "I'll give you two then to start off with just in case you want some more," to which the child responds with a clear "No!" The woman then concedes, "Just one then." The female narrator's voice returns, stating, "Harley Evans lives in Runcorn. There's no battle here over fruit and veg. He's reached the grand old age of three without his mum ever having cooked him a healthy meal." The scene shifts to a street view where a woman in a black coat carries a yellow container of fries. Back inside, the woman in the white tank top hands a drink to the child, who is now eating fries from the yellow container, while she continues to eat pizza. The child's voice is heard saying, "A milkshake," and "And look, come with it," followed by the woman's voice saying, "It's yellow," and the child responding, "Banana," to which the woman confirms, "Yeah." The atmosphere is casual and domestic, focusing on family meals and interactions in a lived-in home environment.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens in a cozy, lived-in home with warm, natural lighting, where a young boy, later identified as Harley, in a blue sweater sits on a black leather couch, actively eating pizza and fries from a red and white checkered pizza box. He babbles and shouts 'Naa, nee, ye-ke!' and later exclaims 'Wow!' as he enjoys his meal, also seen holding a blue sippy cup and a small toy. A male narrator with a British accent details a diet consisting of two portions of doner meat, eight pieces of fried chicken, four portions of chips, two frozen pizzas, and 20 frozen nuggets weekly, along with 24 glasses of fizzy drink, equating to two half-kilo packets of sugar. The narrator explains this diet is because 'he's eating what his mum eats.' The scene shifts briefly to a kitchen where a woman with purple hair interacts with Harley near a refrigerator adorned with colorful magnetic letters and numbers. Back in the living room, a woman, later identified as Taylor, with dark hair, wearing a white tank top and a black feathered shawl, sits on the same black leather couch. She opens the pizza box and eats a slice while Harley, energetic and curious, plays nearby, climbing on the couch and exploring the space. Taylor, speaking with a British accent, discusses her preference for fast food, stating, 'Can I have a small doner meat with chips, with cheese and mayonnaise please?' and explaining it's 'quicker, easier... Someone's made it for you, you don't have to stand around waiting for it to cook... it tastes nice... Cuz they're not a bother to cook.' She adds that she 'usually order[s] a kebab every night,' sometimes KFC. The setting also reveals a high chair with a red patterned backrest and a play tent in the background. The narrator concludes by revealing, 'Team mum Taylor had Harley when she was just 15.'

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a small, fluffy black and white dog barking energetically behind a rusted metal gate in a garden, as somber instrumental music begins to play. The scene transitions to a cozy living room where a woman with dark hair, wearing a zebra-print headband, shares fries and dipping sauce from a takeaway box with a young boy in a blue shirt on a black leather couch. A female narrator explains that "Taylor should know more than most what her diet could be doing to Harley," adding that "Last year, she suffered a heart attack." The camera zooms in on the fries and sauce, highlighting the casual, intimate moment. The video then introduces Taylor, a woman with vibrant purple hair, wearing a gray blazer, who speaks directly to the camera from a well-decorated room with floral wallpaper. She emotionally recounts, "They couldn't find any known explanation for it... the only thing that they can put it down to was all the stress of pregnancy," adding, "I wouldn't ever, ever want to go through that ever again," and describing it as "the worst four days of my life." The scene alternates between Taylor speaking and the dark-haired woman savoring fries. Another woman with blonde hair and glasses then appears, speaking with concern, "I think even the doctors and everyone was shocked with her age, with her being so young, you just don't expect it to happen." Taylor is later seen in a striped armchair, eating pizza from a box and Haribo candies from a bag, with a yellow bowl and stroller visible in the background. The narrator reiterates, "Taylor has been told to change her diet to avoid any more stress to her heart." Taylor then states, "If I carry on eating the way I am, I probably won't be here in 10 years time." The blonde-haired woman expresses her concern again, "Obviously, I think we have arguments because I am concerned about Taylor. If she carries on, it could lead to another heart attack. That could leave her children without..." as the audio fades. The final scenes return to Taylor speaking and the young boy alone on the couch, playing with a yellow plate and purple cushion, maintaining a relaxed, domestic atmosphere throughout the serious discussion.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of a baby in a pink and white checkered bouncer, smiling and playing with a colorful toy, as a child's voice exclaims "Look!". A female narrator then introduces a "tragedy that Maddie hopes can be avoided if Taylor takes action right now," as the scene widens to show a woman with dark hair and a zebra-print headband sharing fries with a young boy in a blue shirt on a black leather couch. Upbeat acoustic guitar music begins as the setting shifts to an outdoor suburban street with terraced brick houses. A woman, identified as Taylor, in a red long-sleeve top and black jacket, arrives at a house with a blue bag. She is greeted at the red-framed front door by a woman in a white hoodie with 'Smile' printed on it, who introduces herself as Haley, the dietitian, saying "Hi, Taylor, I'm Haley, I'm the dietitian." Taylor responds, "Hello, come in. Hi, I've come to see you." The narrator explains that Taylor is seeing community dietitian Haley Kooter, who specializes in pediatric nutrition. Inside the living room with floral wallpaper, Taylor places her bag down and interacts with a young boy in a red plaid shirt. Haley measures the boy's height using a wooden and blue height-measuring device, asking him, "Can you see that number? That shows you what a big boy you are," followed by a child's giggle. A female narrator highlights that "One in five children in the UK are now overweight by the age of five." The scene cuts to an interview-style shot of a woman in a red polka-dot top speaking in front of a cityscape window, warning that "A junk food generation is a ticking time bomb for a really worrying amount of problems." She continues, describing "a lot of overweight and obese children at the moment," and health issues like "tooth decay, constipation, rickets even, from lack of vitamin D," all "caused by an unbalanced diet." The video returns to the living room where Taylor continues to engage with the boy, adjusting the height-measuring device, before ending with a wide shot of the row of terraced houses as the music fades.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a cozy living room, where Hailey, a woman with vibrant purple hair wearing a white hoodie with a pink 'Smile' graphic, sits on a brown leather couch with teal cushions, discussing a child's diet. She states, "according to this BMI, it is above average, and we're gonna need to watch his food intake, really," and mentions, "These takeaways, um, he's hardly having as well." Beside her, a woman with dark hair and a dark blue top, seated on the same couch, takes notes. She adds, "Everything I've got, he wants," and later admits, "I don't think I've ever said he can't, I just give it to him," when Hailey asks if she thinks the boy copies her. A young boy with light hair, wearing a red and black plaid shirt, sits between them, holding a pacifier and looking around curiously. Upbeat, slightly jazzy background music begins as a narrator identifies Hailey as being "most concerned about the enormous amount of sugary cola that Harley is consuming," confirming the boy's name is Harley. The scene shifts to a close-up of Harley’s feet in green and white sneakers as he stands on a wooden floor, then to a low-angle shot from inside a refrigerator as he reaches for a snack, with a bottle of alcohol and colorful containers visible. Harley is then seen on a black leather couch, drinking from a red KFC cup, with the woman with dark hair smiling beside him. The setting transitions to a dining area with floral wallpaper and green curtains, where Harley stands at a wooden table with black placemats. Hailey and the woman with dark hair prepare a mixture in a glass bowl, adding flour and a dark liquid. Harley, asking "Can I have it all?" and "Can I have a little bit more?" as he reaches into the bowl, mixes the contents with his hand, guided by the women. Hailey instructs him, "Keep going," while the woman with dark hair encourages, "Go for it, Harley knows," as sounds of mixing accompany the activity. The atmosphere is warm and familial, with the women nurturing Harley's participation in the playful cooking activity.

segments.integrated_caption: In a warmly lit dining room adorned with floral wallpaper and teal curtains, a collaborative cooking session unfolds. The Woman with dark hair and black top, wearing a dark blue top, guides the Young boy in red plaid shirt, initially addressed as "Mr. Harley," as she pours flour from a glass bowl onto a digital scale. She notes, "It's about 150 grams there," while the boy, holding a green sippy cup, interjects, "I need that sugar." She firmly responds, "No." The Woman with purple hair, in a white hoodie with a pink 'Smile' graphic, observes and occasionally assists, using a spoon to add ingredients. Light, slightly melancholic background music begins as a narrator's voice explains that "Harley is having almost four times the amount of sugar that is recommended for his age," adding that "he's just a mini version of his mum." The Woman with dark hair then asks the boy about his sugar intake, to which he replies, "No, I think it would be more." She encourages him to show how much more, and he exclaims, "Jeez." As the boy becomes more involved, reaching into the bowl and mixing with his hands, he states, "After this, I, I want to go again." The Woman with dark hair responds, "Okay," and then tells him, "I'm afraid you're having a bit more than that." She asks, "Should we try again?" and the Woman with purple hair chimes in with "Yeah?" The Woman with dark hair emphasizes, "It's just a day. This is in one day." The boy asks, "And you want me to keep going?" to which she replies, "Keep going," prompting an "Oh my god" from him. Sounds of pouring and mixing accompany the activity. The Woman with dark hair then declares, "That's 500 grams, Taylor, in one day," now referring to the boy as "Taylor." The narrator's voice concludes, "Taylor is having ten times the recommended daily amount for a grown..." A bottle of milk and a printed recipe sheet are visible on the table, underscoring the structured nature of the cooking. The camera occasionally zooms in on the bowl, highlighting the hands-on activity within the cozy, domestic setting.

Hailey then asks why she eats "like this," to which the Woman with purple hair attributes it to "laziness," expressing interest in eating "healthily" if it means she "could save money and get more stuff." The table is set with a bottle of milk, a green sippy cup, and a printed recipe sheet, all within a cozy domestic room featuring floral wallpaper, teal curtains, and framed artwork of green flowers. The camera alternates between close-ups of the flour and wider shots of their interaction before shifting to a young boy, Harley, with light hair, wearing a blue cardigan, sitting on a brown leather couch with teal cushions, focused on a blue toy. The scene then transitions to an outdoor suburban street at dusk, showing terraced brick houses and a large arched bridge over a river, as the Woman with purple hair is seen standing outside a house with a red-framed front door, smiling and speaking with resolve. She states that it "is going to be hard, but I'm going to try my best," and that she's "just not going to buy any stuff in" for herself and Harley, emphasizing she'll "have to be really strict with myself," before the camera pans to a wide shot of the bridge and surrounding neighborhood.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a woman with shoulder-length brown hair, wearing a black coat and a light gray scarf, standing outdoors in a foggy, overcast environment. She speaks directly to the camera in a calm, reflective voice, discussing how people have "forgotten that food and health are connected," and how someone has "no idea that the sugar, the fat, the saturated fats, the salt that she's putting in her body... is having any effect whatsoever on her health." Somber background music begins as the scene transitions to a moving vehicle shot, showing a blur of residential buildings and trees. Inside the car, the same woman is seen driving, focused on the road, her reflection visible in the rearview mirror. A female narrator's voice explains, "In Cardiff, Sam Fish is facing a huge struggle to get Cuba on the right nutritional track," adding that "His current diet is likely to cause anemia, which in some cases can slow down a child's development." The camera then shifts to the back seat, where a young boy, identified as Cuba, with curly hair, wearing a gray puffer jacket and blue jeans, sits in a car seat with a blue pacifier, looking around curiously. A man's voice, likely Sam Fish, speaks with a Welsh accent, expressing, "It's hitting home, the effect that his diet that we've allowed him to have is affecting him. Now he's got to have a blood test now, he could be anemic. It's obviously affecting his health. It does make me feel a bit guilty." The woman exits the car and walks briskly down a wet street, carrying Cuba, passing parked cars and brick buildings, arriving at a building with a blue awning labeled 'Meadow Sure Start Children’s Centre.' Inside, Cuba is seen playing with a colorful bead maze toy at a small table. The woman, now wearing a leopard-print coat, interacts with Cuba and a blonde woman in a white uniform, who is a nurse. The nurse, speaking gently and reassuringly, tells Cuba, "Right, okay, I'm just gonna pop this little strap on his arm, doesn't hurt, it's just a bit of pressure, okay? Just pop this on a minute, mate. Pull it nice and tight, okay? There we are. What a good boy. segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a bright, modern kitchen with white cabinetry and black countertops, where a woman, identified by a narrator as Sam, in a gray sleeveless sweater and black pants, crouches to retrieve items from a lower cabinet. She then stands, gesturing and speaking in a British accent about food items like "raisins covered in yogurts" and "breadsticks," engaging in a conversation with a child who interjects, "Don't all believe in food." Sam responds, "Well, if you're hungry, that's what's there," and continues describing "oat biscuits," to which the child suggests, "You should get chocolate digestives." The scene briefly cuts to two young girls in a different kitchen setting—one in a dark top sitting on a counter, the other in a blue top laughing—before returning to Sam in the main kitchen. A narrator explains that Sam is "facing up to her fears of a messy kitchen" and has been "cooking fresh meals." Sam opens a refrigerator, revealing various food items, and then moves to a sink area where she handles a bowl of chopped vegetables. A man, identified by the narrator as Simon, in a black polo shirt appears, unpacking a large black bag on a kitchen island, placing items like a red container and a white tray inside. The narrator adds that "Simon is finding it hard to cut out the junk" and "yesterday, he was in charge of the kids." Sam, sounding frustrated, confronts Simon, stating, "No, you didn't make an effort, Simon. I was nearly in tears when I come home from work Friday. I was really, really deflated with you." Simon calmly interjects, "I was the first time," and later, "But I enjoyed my takeaway." Sam continues to argue about the realism of getting rid of "everything" and the responsibility for diet, concluding, "So we've got to take responsibility for that, Simon." As Sam continues her tasks at the sink, occasionally glancing toward Simon, gentle, reflective background music begins, underscoring the domestic and active atmosphere of meal preparation and family dynamics.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of Harley, a young boy in a blue cartoon-print shirt, seated in a dental chair, undergoing an examination. A female narrator explains that 'children under five are admitted to hospital every year with severe tooth decay.' A dentist, wearing a black shirt and white gloves, uses a dental mirror and probe to inspect Harley’s teeth, calmly explaining that he sees 'little marks' which are 'very early signs of decay developing in the teeth,' attributing them to 'sugary drinks.' Taylor, a woman with dark hair styled in a braid, attentively observes. The dentist praises Harley, saying, 'Well done Harley.' Uplifting, slightly melancholic instrumental music begins as the narrator reveals, 'It's a massive jolt for Taylor, and a hard lesson about the harm she's doing to Harley.' The scene transitions to Taylor and Harley walking hand-in-hand down a city sidewalk with another woman, passing a 'Gillman Hill' sign. They enter a home, where Taylor speaks directly to the camera in a kitchen with wooden cabinets and a refrigerator adorned with children’s drawings. With a slightly emotional tone and a Northern English accent, she expresses, 'It's shocked me really what the dentist said,' acknowledging she knew it would be 'harming Harley's teeth' but didn't realize 'how much it was affecting his teeth.' She resolves to 'cut drinks out altogether other than water or milk,' adding, 'And the same for me as well.' Later, Taylor is seen sitting on a black leather couch, opening a bright green bag of snacks with a distinct rustling sound. Harley, now wearing a black jacket and a blue pacifier, takes the snacks and makes a small sound as he begins to eat. The setting shifts to a living room with floral wallpaper, where Harley sits on the floor, playing with a pink pacifier and a remote control, while Taylor watches him from a brown leather couch, as the narrator concludes, 'Taylor finally agrees to take expert advice on board and gets started.'

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a woman in a green coat and purple scarf standing outdoors in a residential area, visually speaking directly to the camera under an overcast sky, amidst terraced brick houses and trimmed hedges. A female narrator's voice discusses changing diets and the need for support groups for mothers in similar positions, mentioning 'Taylor' being inspired to attend 'Parent Power'. The scene transitions to a young woman in a white hoodie and blue jeans walking across a parking lot towards a brick building, identified by a sign as the 'HALTON YOUTH SERVICE Grangeway Youth Club'. Inside, a bright, active kitchen is bustling with several women in aprons preparing food, accompanied by light, uplifting background music. A woman with red hair and another with dark hair are seen assembling pizzas on a table, adding toppings like cheese, spinach, and mushrooms. A participant woman's voice explains that they meet every Friday afternoon to cook healthy alternatives to popular foods like pizzas, kebabs, and cakes. Another woman's voice interjects, "Just cook it all," followed by a man's voice admitting, "I've tried," and humorously adding, "Yeah, no, we set the house on fire." He continues, expressing his belief that he can't cook but would probably try if he did. The first participant woman's voice then details their diverse cooking, including spaghetti bolognese, roast dinners, and "healthy option pizzas." A close-up shows a young child with blonde hair and blue eyes, wearing a gray shirt, being held by an adult and eating, with food visible on their face, embodying the communal, nurturing atmosphere focused on cooking and child care within the youth service center. The man's voice concludes, "Looks like I just got out into me garden and decided."

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a vibrant, brightly lit kitchen or activity room at the Halton Youth Service Grangeway Youth Club, filled with background chatter and the sound of a woman saying, "make a pizza." A young boy in a blue and black checkered shirt is seen reaching for ingredients on a white table covered with food items, including cookies and a bowl of red sauce. A clear female narrator's voice explains that "Taylor's freshly prepared pizza contains around 470 calories," contrasting it with "Harley's usual delivered variety" which "would tot up a massive 3,000." The camera pans to show several women in black aprons with 'The Fun Food Chef' logo, assembling pizzas on white trays. One woman with dark hair and a black and white striped top carefully places toppings like cheese, spinach, mushrooms, and green onions onto a pizza base, while another woman with red hair, wearing a gray top and a black apron, also adds ingredients. The table is labeled with 'SUPPER • LUNCH • DINNER' and features bowls of fresh greens. Colorful posters, including 'Healthy Eating' and 'PARENT POWER', decorate the walls. The scene shifts to a close-up of a woman with dark hair and a braid, who speaks to the camera in a reflective voice, holding a piece of food. She shares her experience, stating she's been asked to come to different groups like this, where she feels people might judge her for being a "young single mum." She explains that before coming here, she "used to just order out" and would "sit eating like pasta and stuff like that." Another woman's voice briefly interjects, "Don't open it!" before the first woman continues, expressing that she "would have never actually sat and thought, yeah, let's try this." She adds that she'll "definitely come again" because it's "something I can take home with me," allowing her and "Harley" to "actually sit and say, 'Oh, I made my tea, I made this.'" Another voice confirms, "You made yours!" The woman with the braid concludes that it will "bring us closer together in a healthy way." The video then transitions to an outdoor shot of a suburban neighborhood with terraced brick houses under a blue sky with scattered clouds, as melancholic, jazzy acoustic guitar music begins to play. The final scene shows a woman in a black top and polka-dot skirt placing a baby in a gray shirt onto a black leather couch in a living room filled with toys and a colorful play tent, while a child's voice sings, "You can't eat some breakfast," followed by a woman saying, "Okay," and the narrator concluding with "Inside..."

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a narrator introducing Kara and Gareth, who are trying to feed their 19-month-old son, Michael, healthy home-cooked food. Visually, Kara, in a black top and polka-dot skirt, places Michael, wearing a gray onesie, onto a black leather couch, adjusting cushions before offering him a green sippy cup. Kara's voice expresses concern, noting, "He hasn't touched his toast," and later, "Look at this mess, look at this mess, Michael," as she tries to encourage him to eat "nona." The narrator explains that since contracting meningitis a year ago, Michael's parents have become overcontrolling at mealtimes, leading to Michael's rebellion. The scene briefly shifts to Gareth, in a checkered shirt, clapping as he eats at a dining table, then to him pushing a stroller with a child on a suburban sidewalk. Upbeat, whimsical music begins as the narrator reveals Michael has been referred to feeding expert Lucy Thomas for practical support for Kara and Gareth. The video transitions to a brightly lit children’s center, where Lucy Thomas, in a red long-sleeve top, dances energetically before kneeling to interact with Michael, now in a gray sweater and blue bib, engaging him playfully with a cucumber. Gareth, in a black leather jacket, watches and smiles. Lucy Thomas's clear voice explains her classes are about offering parents with young children the opportunity to explore and experiment with fruit and vegetables, emphasizing the importance of feeling and smelling food rather than just putting it straight into their mouth, making it a fun and positive experience for Michael. The scene concludes with Lucy clapping as Michael walks toward her, and Gareth gently guiding him while Lucy continues her enthusiastic encouragement, exclaiming, "Fantastic!" The center's walls display educational posters like 'Beetroot' and 'yellow pepper', and colorful bowls of produce are visible on the checkered floor.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of Michael, an infant in a blue bib with a green cartoon character, being fed apple slices by his father, Gareth, who wears a black and white striped shirt. A narrator introduces Michael's 'mini triumph' as Gareth enthusiastically describes an activity Michael attended as 'brilliant,' stating Michael 'really took to it really well' and will be 'coming every week now.' The scene transitions to an outdoor setting where Gareth, now in a black jacket and patterned scarf, pushes Michael in a stroller, who wears a brown hooded coat. Upbeat acoustic guitar music begins to play as the camera captures a dramatic sunset, followed by a clear blue sky over a brick house. Inside, the narrator explains that Gareth is trying to bring the 'classroom fun into the home.' Gareth, in a gray shirt, makes a 'Woo!' sound before assisting Michael, now in a high chair with sliced cucumbers and beetroot, placing a sippy cup on the table. Gareth encourages Michael, asking, 'You're going to eat it with Daddy now, yeah?' accompanied by tapping sounds. Michael, wearing a gray jacket and green bib, is then seen standing on a blue rug in a living room, playing with toys, before being picked up and carried by Gareth. Gareth continues to encourage Michael, saying, 'You're gonna come back, have some more. Come on, please, come on.' Michael responds with a soft vocalization and a distinct 'No' when offered more food. Gareth playfully makes an 'Mmm-mmm' sound and asks, 'When you brush your teeth?' before commenting, 'You don't like any of it. You did the other day,' with a questioning 'Hmm?' The interaction is warm and playful, set against a homey living space with a black leather couch, a white desk with a computer, and framed photos on the window ledge.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a warm living room where Gareth, a man in a gray button-up shirt, sits on the floor with his young son, Michael, who wears a gray hoodie and a green bib. Gareth offers Michael a slice of cucumber from a white plate of sliced apples, cucumbers, and red peppers, saying "have a bite." Michael makes a "buh" sound and sighs, prompting Gareth to remark to Kara, "this isn't going down well," and "He's spat nearly every bit out." Kara responds, "Are you joking?" A narrator explains that "Michael's making small changes, but his parents are not." Gareth expresses his disinterest in the food, stating, "I'm not keen on that anyway," and that it's a "matter of confidence for me doing it as well." Michael then drinks from a green sippy cup, guided by Gareth, but soon begins to cry loudly. The narrator adds that "Kara and Gareth's relationship is still suffering, and mealtimes are still chaos." Kara asks Michael, "Do you want to go upstairs to bed?" while Gareth encourages him, "Come on, be a good boy." The scene transitions to a kitchen where Michael, now standing, peers into a sink cabinet, revealing cleaning supplies, while Gareth, in a green shirt, smiles at the camera. Kara, with dark hair tied back and wearing a denim shirt, kneels by a washing machine to interact with Michael. The narrator then states, "Thankfully, their first consultation with Dr. Katherine is scheduled." The setting shifts outdoors to a sidewalk where Gareth and Kara, dressed in winter coats, walk hand-in-hand before entering a cozy pub. Seated at a wooden table, Gareth in a black sweater and Kara in a leopard-print scarf, they engage in a serious conversation. Kara asks, "What particularly is it that that worries you?" Gareth responds, expressing concern that Michael "is not going to reach his milestones and maybe miss out on things and be a bit slow." Kara concludes, "I get really scared when he doesn't eat enough."

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens in a warmly lit pub, featuring large windows overlooking a snowy outdoor scene, where three individuals are seated around a rustic wooden table. The Woman in purple top, with short blonde hair, speaks animatedly, discussing someone getting "thinner and thinner" and "so hungry," going to "starve," calling it "silly." Beside her, the Woman in black top with leopard-print scarf, wearing a black headband and gold hoop earrings, smiles and gestures, while the Man in black sweater, with curly light brown hair, laughs, covering his mouth. The Woman in black top with leopard-print scarf then reflects on being "right there with the emotion" when someone was ill. The Man in black sweater, who later holds a small object between his fingers, agrees, stating one tends to "feel worse," prompting further agreement from the women. The Woman in black top with leopard-print scarf questions if someone is "unhappy" and if they "look at those got energy," to which the Man in black sweater responds with a "Yeah" and a laugh. She continues, asking if they are "reaching his milestones." The Man in black sweater then remarks on their silliness, saying "we know he's all this is..." before the Woman in purple top interjects, "We know he's okay, like." The Woman in black top with leopard-print scarf concludes this segment by suggesting they are "too quick to get stressed" and "start fretting." Throughout this lively interaction, the camera pans and zooms between them. Playfully, the Woman in purple top then stands and lifts the table, revealing the Man in black sweater and the Woman in black top with leopard-print scarf crouched underneath. The scene then cuts to a close-up of a Woman with dark hair and black top, in a different setting with patterned wallpaper, who speaks directly to the camera. Her clear, informative narration, accompanied by gentle background music, explains that "Children are naturally suspicious of food and use all their senses to work out what's okay to eat," before she announces, "We're going to play a game together, and I hope it's going to be fun." The overall atmosphere is casual, humorous, and social.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a warmly lit, spacious room with hardwood floors, where a woman in a purple top, with short blonde hair and glasses, leads a tasting activity at a rustic wooden table. She speaks animatedly, explaining in a clear, articulate voice that the activity is about experiencing how 'Michael feels' when presented with unknown food, and hopes 'Kara and Gareth' (the woman in the leopard-print scarf and the man in the black sweater, respectively) will understand. She then warns them that 'some of them are not edible,' prompting the man in the black sweater to express concern, stating, 'It's quite worrying when you say some of them aren't edible.' The camera cuts to close-ups of hands placing small aluminum cups with dark liquids onto a black circular tray. The participants smell and taste the samples, with thoughtful, curious, and amused expressions. The woman in purple asks, 'So would you eat that?' to which both the man and the woman in the scarf respond with laughter and a firm 'No, I wouldn't eat that.' The woman in the scarf comments that one sample 'smells foody.' Laughter fills the convivial atmosphere as they continue tasting. The woman in purple then puts them 'out of their misery,' revealing the liquid is 'an iron tonic.' The woman in the scarf confirms, 'It does smell irony and taste a bit irony,' with the man agreeing. The woman in purple concludes by setting up a hypothetical scenario for 'Michael,' asking what he would do if told to 'eat that, it's good for you.'

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a serene suburban street scene at golden hour, featuring cars driving past leafless trees and terraced houses. The setting then shifts to a grocery store where Simon, an adult man in a red and blue jacket, is heard saying he will "treat them with some cakes." A female narrator reveals that "Simon is sneaking in snacks behind Sam's back" as he pushes a stroller into the store. Simon then asks the girls, "But we don't tell Mummy, do we, girls?" to which a child's voice responds, "No." As a woman in a black jacket walks past a bakery display featuring 'flake fancy' priced at 53p, Simon asks, "What cookies are we having?" and a girl excitedly replies, "Cake, cake, cake, yeah." Simon continues to interact with the girls about their choices, asking "Which ones do you want? You got everything?" and a child confirms, "Yeah," before he urges, "Come on, let's get back to the car quick." Inside a car, a young child with curly hair in a gray puffer jacket is seen in a car seat, holding a wooden toy, while a woman in a pink and white striped shirt sits beside them. Simon's voice is heard asking, "Enjoying that?" and then describing his own treat, "I got a chocolate cake with cream I have. I can't wait." A peaceful shot of birds silhouetted on a rooftop against a warm sunset sky follows. The scene transitions to a bright, domestic kitchen with white cabinets, a fruit basket, and floral curtains, where Simon, now in a gray hoodie with yellow text, is on the phone. Upbeat, slightly jazzy background music begins as Simon's voice is heard discussing food, saying he "changed the chicken tikka for another lamb pathanda piece." A female narrator explains, "Back at home, there's more fast food on the menu, as the Fish family are tucking into their one weekly takeaway." A woman in a red top unpacks groceries, placing bread and eggs from a blue bag onto the counter, while a young girl in a red school uniform eats at the table. Another young girl in a green top joins her, helping prepare food. A child's voice is heard saying "Happy birthday," and Simon offers, "Right, let's get you some more chips. Yes, more chips for all week." The narrator clarifies that the takeaway "is meant to be there as a reward for all their good work." Simon then remarks, "We haven't had one of these for ages now, we girls, since Jane's come along," to which a child responds, "No," before Simon's voice is cut off saying, "Jane's..." segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of Simon, a man in a gray t-shirt with a graphic print, driving and holding a piece of fried food, as a voice-over introduces a "large size bucket of fried chicken in lightning quick time." Simon remarks, "I think this is gonna be really hard giving up this," and confirms he "couldn't resist" when prompted by a woman's voice. The scene transitions to a bright, modern kitchen where a woman in a black cardigan over a yellow top and another woman in a red top with a black cardigan and colorful scarf are unpacking a blue plastic bag, pouring golden fries and placing fried chicken pieces onto a white paper-lined plate. As Simon, now seated at the counter, takes a large bite of the chicken, the woman in the yellow top asks if it's "appealing," to which Simon replies it "smells nice," though the woman in the red top interjects that it "doesn't smell nice to me, it smells very very greasy really." They discuss the quantity, with the woman in the yellow top identifying him as "Simon" and noting "16 pieces of chicken," questioning if he'd normally have bought "this amount." The woman in the yellow top then exclaims, "Oh, gross," and encourages, "Look, you can actually squeeze it out." As one of the women breaks open a piece of fried chicken, revealing its juicy interior and crispy texture, the woman in the red top points out the grease "bubbling to the surface." Simon agrees it's "pretty gross," and the woman in the yellow top describes "Rivers of grease running down my hand," concluding, "This should not be your daily diet." Somber music then begins as a narrator states, "This is food gorged in harmful fat," while the kitchen, illuminated by warm under-cabinet lighting and featuring a stainless steel range hood, maintains a relaxed, domestic atmosphere centered on the food and the group's critical discussion.

segments.integrated_caption: The video captures a warm, intimate family meal in a cozy, well-lit restaurant with modern decor, including hanging pendant lights and chandeliers. A young child with light hair, wearing a plaid shirt, is seated in a high chair at a wooden dining table, actively eating a piece of cucumber and later a sandwich, making audible eating sounds. Adults encourage him with phrases like “That’s it” and “Good boy.” A woman with short blonde hair in a purple top expressively describes how “He’s just picked up the biggest bit of broccoli and put it in his mouth and he’s munching away.” Laughter from the adults fills the air as a woman with dark hair and a pink flower headband, who is smiling and interacting with the child, adds that “He’ll eat mounds of broccoli.” A man in a black and white patterned sweater agrees, noting “He’ll delve right in,” and comments that the child is “like a different boy at the moment,” which the woman with dark hair affirms. The woman in the purple top then shifts to a more serious tone, discussing the “big change” and the previously unspoken memories of the child being diagnosed with meningitis and going into the hospital, describing it as “understandably really upsetting.” She explains that they “never really openly talked about his meningitis before,” but after doing so, they “went home and talked about it ourselves.” The man in the patterned sweater adds that it’s “a big help just to get things out,” feeling they were “bottling it up too much,” while the woman with dark hair agrees. The camera closely captures the child’s curious expressions and actions, then pans to the adults, emphasizing their convivial conversation amidst the restaurant’s ambient lighting and decor, with plates of food like green puree and steamed broccoli visible on the table.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens in a warmly lit pub or restaurant, where the Woman in black top with pink flower headband listens intently, resting her chin on her hand, as the Man in black sweater with white geometric pattern speaks animatedly about someone picking up on everything and not having problems. A woman's voice, later identified as Taylor, comments that "you two together look much happier." The man, with a clear, conversational male voice, agrees they were "stressed all the time," to which Taylor confirms, "Yeah, definitely." She continues, explaining that "Now, we're relaxed with each other and don't argue as much," prompting the man to interject "As much!" before both share a hearty laugh. Taylor then reveals, "It helps because we're getting married," followed by more laughter and another voice exclaiming "Great!" The scene briefly cuts to a close-up of a man in a plaid shirt before returning to the main conversation. The camera then shifts to the Woman in purple top, with short blonde hair, who speaks expressively, gesturing with her hands, her distinct female voice advising that "in the future you're going to need to really be firm, not only with Michael, but actually with yourselves as well." The Man in black sweater adds, "I think we've learned a lot ourselves," with Taylor agreeing, "Definitely, yeah," and the man concluding, "I think we'll be able to do it," to which Taylor affirms, "We'll do it." Upbeat, light background music begins as the scene transitions to a domestic kitchen with black-and-white photo wallpaper. A female narrator's voice announces, "In Runcorn, it's a week after the cooking class, and Taylor is for the first time in her life starting to cook at home." The Woman in black top with pink flower headband, now in a dark floral dress, is preparing a snack with the Young child with curly hair, wearing a gray shirt. They sit at a glass table with colorful plastic bowls filled with chopped vegetables and corn. The child, identified by the narrator as Harley, asks, "What are you doing, babe?" An adult male voice (likely off-screen) responds, "What am I doing? I'm cutting up tomatoes." The narrator adds that "It's healthier for her heart, and healthier for Harley." The child reaches for a red packet, showing curiosity, while Taylor arranges the vibrant bowls of carrots, beans, and corn. The scene ends with a close-up of Taylor’s hands as she places a pink plate on the table, completing the snack setup, as the adult male voice continues, "That's a go, and now..."

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a woman with blonde hair and glasses, wearing a white top, in a room adorned with Marilyn Monroe photo wallpaper. The scene quickly shifts to a kitchen where a woman in a dark floral dress is preparing "super healthy pizzas" on a glass table, using colorful plastic bowls filled with ingredients like corn, diced tomatoes, and chicken. An adult male voice remarks, "Wow, I'm shocked at your mum making her own pizzas like this," identifying the floral dress woman as the boy's mother. A young boy with curly blonde hair, wearing a gray shirt, is actively involved, eagerly responding "Yeah!" to the male voice's instructions and pouring sauce from a red bottle onto a pizza base. The woman then places the prepared pizzas on a baking tray and puts them into an oven, commenting in a British accent, "I used to think I couldn't cook, but it's evidently clear that I can cook," and expressing pride, "I deserve like a certificate or something, like a medal." After baking, she removes the pizzas, prompting the boy to exclaim "Oh, the door!" with excited yells, and serves them to him. He is now seated at the glass table, eagerly eating his pizza with his hands, while the adult male voice asks, "Good, isn't it?", to which the boy echoes, "Good, isn't it?". The woman with blonde hair and glasses reappears, holding a baby in a pink outfit, as they sit together at the table. The boy continues to eat his pizza, looking up with a joyful expression, while the woman in the floral dress interacts with him. A female narrator's voice comments on the boy's progress, stating, "His diet still isn't perfect, but he's cut down over half his sugar intake and is a much calmer child," adding that he "doesn't seem as bad as he was in the beginning, probably because he's not getting as much junk in." The setting is cozy and domestic, with natural light coming through a window, and the atmosphere is warm and familial.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a child’s bedroom with soccer-ball-themed wallpaper, where Sam, an adult woman in a yellow top and colorful floral skirt, gently tucks her young son, Cuba, into his wooden crib with matching soccer-patterned bedding. Cuba, wearing a white polka-dot shirt and blue jeans, has a blue pacifier. A female narrator explains that someone was "severely anemic" and they "nipped it in the bud" by changing an "old diet." Sam playfully engages with Cuba before lifting him out. The scene transitions to a bright, modern kitchen with white cabinetry and black countertops. The narrator introduces "The Fish family" who have "totally changed their eating habits," noting that "Simon's curbed his fast food cravings" and "Sam is triumphing with her healthy home cooking." Sam, now standing at the kitchen island, speaks directly to the camera, animatedly stating, "I'm cooking every day now, to be honest with you, which is not too bad." In the background, Simon, a man in a striped polo shirt, washes dishes at the sink, while pots of boiling potatoes and steaming carrots are on the stove. Sam then chops green vegetables. The narrator adds that "Cuba's new diet is improving his sleep patterns" and he's "well on the way to beating his anemia." Cuba is then carried into the kitchen, and the family moves together to the grassy backyard. Simon opens a trampoline net, and Cuba joyfully jumps inside, making happy sounds like "Mama, hello! Whoa!" Simon watches with a smile, holding the trampoline’s frame. He then explains that "what we were giving him before with the fried chicken and stuff like that was way too much for him" and they "didn't think it was affecting him as much as it was." The backyard features a clothesline with drying laundry and neighboring houses, creating a warm, domestic atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a young child with curly hair, wearing a white polka-dot shirt and blue jeans, running through a backyard near a trampoline, viewed through its safety netting, as a male voice states, "When you actually see it for yourself then... you're gonna change that," followed by a child's vocalization. A female narrator then mentions "Sam" and her "messy kitchen." The scene shifts to a kitchen where a man in a gray, white, and green striped polo shirt washes potatoes in a sink filled with soapy water, using a metal scrubber, while a male voice, likely his, exclaims, "Look at that! I got to make a mess. What did I say to you, right? That will get washed up," and later adds, "Yeah, but I just don't understand." The narrator continues, stating that "the whole family and of course Cuba, are on the path to a healthier future." The man is then seen cooking on a stove with boiling potatoes and steaming carrots, before serving a meal to a young child with curly hair, now in a blue and gray checkered shirt, seated in a high chair. The child eagerly eats from a colorful plate with a fork as the man pours a brown sauce from a red measuring cup over the mashed potatoes, broccoli, carrots, and meat. A woman with dark hair, wearing a cream-colored textured sweater, is briefly shown seated on a couch with patterned cushions, looking off-camera, as a female voice, likely hers, reflects, "Looking back at it all now, it was nothing to do with Cuba, absolutely nothing. So the problem definitely laid with us... that we didn't take control of, control of our baby, basically. He was controlling us, so it was definitely, definitely with us." Upbeat, rhythmic background music with synth elements begins and continues as the video concludes with a black screen displaying white text production credits, including 'Narrator Hannah Peckham' and 'Filmed, Produced & Directed by Vicki Cooper', alongside a pink 'ORIGIN' logo.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video opens with a young child with curly hair, wearing a white polka-dot shirt and blue jeans, running through a backyard near a trampoline with a black safety net, viewed through the netting. The scene shifts to a kitchen where a man in a gray, white, and green striped polo shirt is washing potatoes in a sink filled with soapy water, using a metal scrubber. He then moves to a stove, where he is seen cooking, with a pot of boiling potatoes and another of steaming sliced carrots visible. The man then serves a meal to a young child with curly hair, wearing a blue and gray checkered shirt, seated in a high chair with a red patterned backrest. The child is eating from a colorful plate with a fork, and the man pours a brown sauce from a red measuring cup over the food, which includes mashed potatoes, broccoli, carrots, and a piece of meat. The child eagerly eats, using the fork to scoop up the food. The scene briefly cuts to a woman with dark hair, wearing a cream-colored textured sweater, seated on a couch with patterned cushions, looking off-camera with a neutral expression. The video ends with a black screen displaying white text credits, including 'Narrator Hannah Peckham', 'Titles Bradley Evans', 'Edit Assistant', 'Researcher Sophie Stone', 'Production Manager Hannah Thomas', 'Unit Manager Isobel Nicholson', 'Online Editor Marc Corrance', 'Dubbing Mixer Andy Hodges', 'Executive Producers for the BBC Sophy Walker, Samantha Anstiss', 'Offline Editors Peter Price, Meg Townend', 'Creative Directors Liz Warner, Walter Iuzzolino', 'Assistant Producers Grace Gibbons, Cheryl Jackson', 'Executive Producer for Bot.tv', and 'Filmed, Produced & Directed by Vicki Cooper', alongside a pink background with the word 'ORIGIN' and a dandelion logo.
